osteopathy.title: Osteopathy
osteopathy.intro: Osteopathy is a regulated allied health profession specialising in musculoskeletal health. Our osteopaths treat a wide range of conditions including back and neck pain, headaches and migraines, joint pain and arthritis, posture imbalances, muscle tightness, pregnancy-related pain, and growth pain — for patients of all ages, from infants to seniors.

specialties.title: Paediatric Osteopathy
specialties.description: Osteopathy is safe and effective for babies, toddlers, and children of all ages. Using gentle, non-invasive techniques, we can help with birth-related discomforts, colic, feeding difficulties, sleep disturbances, growing pains, and postural development. Early treatment supports healthy growth and addresses issues before they become chronic.

specialties.title: Pregnancy Related Pain
specialties.description: Pregnancy places significant demands on the body as it adapts to support new life. Osteopathic treatment can relieve lower back and pelvic pain, sciatica, rib discomfort, and postural strain — with a gentle approach that is safe throughout all trimesters and supports both mother and baby's wellbeing.

specialties.title: Elderly Patients
specialties.description: As we age, joint stiffness, arthritis, reduced mobility and balance issues can affect quality of life. Osteopathic care for older adults uses gentle techniques to improve movement, reduce pain, and support independence — helping manage chronic conditions and maintain an active, fulfilling lifestyle.

specialties.title: Sport Injury
specialties.description: Whether you're a weekend warrior or a competitive athlete, osteopathy can help you recover faster and prevent future injuries. We treat sprains, strains, shoulder and knee pain, and overuse injuries — combining hands-on treatment with personalised rehabilitation advice to get you back to doing what you love.

faq.question: What conditions can osteopathy treat?
faq.answer: Osteopathy can help with back and neck pain, headaches and migraines, joint pain and arthritis, posture imbalances, muscle tightness, pregnancy-related pain, sports injuries, and growth or developmental issues in children. It's suitable for patients of all ages, from infants to seniors.

faq.question: Is osteopathy covered by ACC?
faq.answer: Yes. Our osteopaths are ACC-registered providers, so treatment for an accident-related injury is subsidised by ACC. If you haven't lodged a claim yet, we can help you do so at your first appointment — no GP referral needed.

faq.question: Is osteopathy safe during pregnancy?
faq.answer: Yes. Osteopathic treatment uses gentle techniques that are safe throughout all trimesters. It can relieve lower back and pelvic pain, sciatica, rib discomfort, and postural strain as your body adapts to support your pregnancy.

faq.question: Can osteopaths treat babies and children?
faq.answer: Yes — osteopathy is safe and effective for babies, toddlers, and children of all ages. We use gentle, non-invasive techniques adapted for younger patients to help with birth-related discomforts, colic, feeding difficulties, sleep disturbances, growing pains, and postural development.

acupuncture.title: Acupuncture
acupuncture.intro: Acupuncture at Meridian Osteopathy blends Traditional Chinese Medicine with modern evidence-based practice. Our practitioners treat a wide range of conditions including chronic pain, headaches, stress and anxiety, sleep difficulties, women's health, digestive concerns, and fatigue — supporting your body's natural ability to heal and restore balance.

specialties.title: Pain Relief & Recovery
specialties.description: Acupuncture is highly effective for both acute and chronic pain. By stimulating specific points on the body, it triggers the release of natural pain-relieving chemicals, reduces inflammation, and improves circulation. Whether you're dealing with back pain, joint issues, headaches, or sports injuries, acupuncture offers a drug-free pathway to lasting relief.

specialties.title: Stress, Anxiety & Sleep
specialties.description: In today's fast-paced world, stress and poor sleep take a significant toll on health. Acupuncture helps regulate the nervous system, lower cortisol levels, and promote a deep sense of calm. Regular treatment can improve sleep quality, reduce anxiety, and restore emotional balance — helping you feel grounded and resilient.

specialties.title: Women's Health
specialties.description: Acupuncture has been used for centuries to support women's health across all life stages. From regulating menstrual cycles and easing period pain to managing pregnancy discomforts and navigating perimenopause and menopause, our practitioners provide gentle, holistic care tailored to your unique needs. Infertility acupuncture is one of our most sought-after specialties — research supports its role in improving ovarian function, regulating hormonal cycles, reducing stress, and enhancing uterine lining receptivity. Whether you are trying to conceive naturally or are undergoing IVF or other assisted reproductive treatments, acupuncture can be a powerful complement to your fertility journey, supporting both physical and emotional wellbeing throughout the process.

specialties.title: Digestive & Immune Health
specialties.description: A healthy gut is the foundation of overall wellbeing. Acupuncture can help regulate digestive function, reduce bloating and discomfort, and support conditions such as IBS, reflux, and nausea. It also plays a role in strengthening immune resilience and managing fatigue, helping your body maintain its natural balance.

faq.question: Is acupuncture painful?
faq.answer: Most patients find acupuncture comfortable. The needles are very fine — finer than a hair — and any sensation is usually brief. Many people feel deeply relaxed during treatment.

faq.question: What does acupuncture treat?
faq.answer: Acupuncture is effective for chronic and acute pain, headaches and migraines, stress and anxiety, sleep difficulties, women's health concerns (including menstrual irregularities, fertility, and menopause), digestive complaints, and fatigue. Our practitioners blend traditional Chinese medicine with modern evidence-based approaches.

faq.question: Can acupuncture help with fertility and IVF?
faq.answer: Yes. Research supports acupuncture's role in improving ovarian function, regulating hormonal cycles, reducing stress, and enhancing uterine lining receptivity. It can complement both natural conception and assisted reproductive treatments such as IVF.

faq.question: Is acupuncture covered by ACC?
faq.answer: Yes, when treatment is for an accident-related injury. Our acupuncturists are ACC-registered. If you haven't lodged a claim yet, we can help you do so at your first appointment — no GP referral needed.

herbs.title: Herbal Medicine
herbs.intro: Herbal medicine at Meridian Osteopathy draws on thousands of years of Traditional Chinese Medicine wisdom, combined with contemporary research. Our practitioners create personalised herbal prescriptions using carefully selected plant-based ingredients to address a wide range of conditions — from pain and fatigue to digestive health, hormonal imbalance, and mental wellbeing.

specialties.title: Pain & Inflammation
specialties.description: Herbal formulas have long been used to address pain and reduce inflammation throughout the body. Our practitioners select specific herbs known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, creating personalised blends that work alongside your other treatments to provide sustained relief from chronic pain, arthritis, and musculoskeletal conditions.

specialties.title: Stress, Sleep & Mental Wellbeing
specialties.description: Adaptogenic and calming herbs have a profound ability to support the nervous system, reduce the impact of stress, and promote restful sleep. Our herbal prescriptions are tailored to your individual pattern — whether you're experiencing burnout, anxiety, insomnia, or low mood — to help restore your energy and emotional balance.

specialties.title: Digestive Health
specialties.description: Digestive health is central to overall wellness in Traditional Chinese Medicine. Herbal formulas can help regulate gut function, ease bloating, cramping, and discomfort, and support conditions such as IBS, reflux, and poor appetite. Our practitioners assess your individual presentation to create a formula that addresses the root cause, not just the symptoms.

specialties.title: Women's Health & Hormonal Balance
specialties.description: Herbal medicine is particularly powerful in supporting women's health throughout every life stage. From regulating menstrual cycles and supporting fertility to managing symptoms of perimenopause and menopause, our personalised herbal prescriptions work with your body's natural rhythms to restore hormonal balance and promote lasting wellbeing.

faq.question: What is Chinese herbal medicine?
faq.answer: Traditional Chinese herbal medicine uses carefully balanced plant-based formulas, prescribed individually to your constitution and presenting pattern. It works alongside acupuncture and osteopathy to support healing from the inside out.

faq.question: How are the herbs prescribed?
faq.answer: After a full case history and assessment, your practitioner creates a personalised formula tailored to your specific pattern and concern. You'll typically take the formula as a granule or liquid over several weeks, with the blend adjusted as your condition changes.

faq.question: Are Chinese herbs safe alongside other medications?
faq.answer: In most cases yes, but always tell your practitioner about every medication and supplement you take so they can prescribe safely. Our practitioners are trained to recognise and avoid herb-drug interactions, and will adjust or defer herbs when that's the safer call.

faq.question: What does herbal medicine help with?
faq.answer: Herbal formulas are used for chronic pain and inflammation, digestive complaints, stress, sleep difficulties, low mood, fatigue, women's health (menstrual, fertility, menopausal symptoms), and hormonal imbalances. It's particularly useful for slow-to-resolve or constitutional patterns.
